collections/ChainMap/parents.name: parents
collections/ChainMap/parents.description: Property returning a new ChainMap containing all of the maps in the current instance except the first one. This is useful for skipping the first map in the search. Use cases are similar to those for the nonlocal keyword used in nested scopes. The use cases also parallel those for the built-in super() function. A reference to d.parents is equivalent to: ChainMap(*d.maps[1:]).

info.excerpt: Property returning a new ChainMap containing all of the maps in the current instance except the first one. This is useful for skipping the first map in the search. Use cases are similar to those for t...

collections/deque/pop.name: pop
collections/deque/pop.description: Remove and return an element from the right side of the deque. If no elements are present, raises an IndexError.

info.excerpt: Remove and return an element from the right side of the deque. If no elements are present, raises an IndexError.

collections/OrderedDict/popitem.name: popitem
collections/OrderedDict/popitem.description: The popitem() method for ordered dictionaries returns and removes a (key, value) pair. The pairs are returned in LIFO order if last is true or FIFO order if false.

info.excerpt: The popitem() method for ordered dictionaries returns and removes a (key, value) pair. The pairs are returned in LIFO order if last is true or FIFO order if false.

collections/deque/popleft.name: popleft
collections/deque/popleft.description: Remove and return an element from the left side of the deque. If no elements are present, raises an IndexError.

info.excerpt: Remove and return an element from the left side of the deque. If no elements are present, raises an IndexError.
